excludedFromNewDiscoveryCount.regressionGate: Every commerce response fetch stays below 256000 bytes.
Compatibility document, typed projection, command record and mirror event commit together.
Two deterministic dispatch rows exist per booking and historical unknowns never retry.
Every owedReferral and creatorPayout source has one exact-cent ledger row.
Every provider refund entry has one typed refund row.
Delete stays auditable and create/createIfNotExists can safely recreate the ID.
Commerce can switch while DATA_PRIMARY_BACKEND remains Sanity.

newlyConfirmedAndClosed.reproduction: Payment recovery fields changed in Sanity during the ten-minute parity window and produced nine document-hash drifts.
newlyConfirmedAndClosed.closureGate: A leased keyset cursor atomically imports, projects, verifies and reconciles changed commerce documents every cron run.

newlyConfirmedAndClosed.reproduction: A missing targeted RPC could fall back to the broad compatibility reader.
newlyConfirmedAndClosed.closureGate: Commerce reads now return 503 when their scope or targeted RPC is unavailable, and enforce the 250 KB payload ceiling.

newlyConfirmedAndClosed.reproduction: The relational email ledger was projected, but booking documents still owned the actual send lease.
newlyConfirmedAndClosed.closureGate: Supabase-primary sends atomically claim and complete deterministic customer and owner ledger rows; terminal sends cannot be downgraded.

newlyConfirmedAndClosed.reproduction: A newly created Sanity rate-limit bucket was reported as commerce drift even though rate-limit state is intentionally backend-local.
newlyConfirmedAndClosed.closureGate: Both current and legacy ephemeral bucket types are excluded symmetrically from source and target document parity while the native Supabase limiter remains covered by dedicated tests.
